title: Fix resource leak in test_get_db_returns_catalog_db
analysis: In tests/test_web_deps.py, TestGetDb.test_get_db_returns_catalog_db (lines 28-35) calls get_db(request) which creates a CatalogDB wrapping sqlite3.connect(), but never closes the result. The sibling test test_get_db_uses_app_db_path (lines 37-45) correctly calls result.close() at line 45. The fix is to add try/finally with result.close() to test_get_db_returns_catalog_db, matching the cleanup pattern established by the adjacent test. This is a test-only change — no production code is modified. CatalogDB.__init__ opens a sqlite3 connection (autopilot/db.py), and CatalogDB.close() must be called to release the file descriptor. The existing test file has no fixtures for DB cleanup; both tests manage their own lifecycle inline.

steps.description: Run test_get_db_returns_catalog_db to confirm it currently passes (baseline). Then run it with -W error::ResourceWarning to verify the resource leak is detectable as an unclosed sqlite3 connection warning.

Add try/finally with result.close() to test_get_db_returns_catalog_db: wrap the isinstance assertion in try, call result.close() in finally. This matches the cleanup pattern of the sibling test_get_db_uses_app_db_path. Run the test again with -W error::ResourceWarning to confirm the leak is fixed.

design_decisions.decision: Use try/finally rather than just appending result.close() after the assert
design_decisions.rationale: Ensures the connection is closed even if the assertion fails, preventing fd leaks in failure paths. This is the robust cleanup pattern suggested in the task description.

design_decisions.decision: Match the sibling test's inline cleanup pattern rather than introducing a fixture
design_decisions.rationale: The adjacent test (test_get_db_uses_app_db_path) uses inline result.close() rather than a fixture. Consistency within the test class is preferred. A fixture would be overengineering for two tests with slightly different setup logic.

reuse.how: Apply the same result.close() call, wrapped in try/finally for robustness since the assertion could fail between open and close
